converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Left wing political activities printed material 3650
2.6 cubic feet. 2.6 cubic feet.
1966-2007. Newspapers, broadsides, brochures, clippings, newsletters, pamphlets, notes, and correspondence. Consists primarily of printed items, newspapers, broadsides, brochures, clippings, newsletters, and pamphlets, mostly relating to Cornell University and its student organizations. Also includes notes and correspondence. Collection material in English 2068677
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
T. Hickerson
Date completed:
1977
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, September 2001RMC Staff, January 2008
Date modified:
Jude Corina, December 2013
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Consists primarily of printed items, newspapers, broadsides, brochures, clippings, newsletters, and pamphlets, relating to Cornell University and its student organizations and other organizations. Includes notes and correspondence. Topics documented include the Black Panther Party; Bobby Seale; Collegetown disturbance, Ithaca, New York, May 13, 1972; Cornell Aeronautical Laboratory (CAL); Giap-Cabral Community; Ithaca Labor Committee; May Day 1971; New American Movement; New Haven 9; the occupation of Carpenter Hall, Cornell University; Students for a Democratic Society; TIOHERO, leaflets gathered in San Francisco, 2007-2008. A number of circa 1972 materials, some published by Glad Day Press, from Ithaca Anti-War Coalition, Peoples Coalition for Peace and Justice, Ithaca United Action Against Imperialism, Peoples Anti-Fascist Resistance League, Southern African Liberation Committee, Ad hoc Committee to Combat Cornell Complicity, and the Committee of Concerned Asian Scholars at Cornell. Poster for The Red Detachment of Women event sponsored by the New China Study Group, China Program Students.
Cite As:
Left Wing Political Activities Printed Material, #3650. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
College Students -- Political activity. Radicalism. Political clubs. May Day (Labor holiday) Tiohero. Seale, Bobby, 1936- Black Panther Party Cornell Aeronautical Laboratory Ithaca Labor Committee New American Movement (Organization) New Haven Nine Students for a Democratic Society Cornell University -- : Students.

Scope and Contents
Contains: poster from Willard Straight event on the trial of Daniel Berrigan and Bruce Dancis; poster for event "We Must Love in the Matrix of War" after the death of George Jackson, September 26, 1971.Revolutionary People's Constitutional Convention, September 4-7, 1970197011Revolutionary People's Constitutional Convention, November 1970197012Fall 19701970-197113Spring 19711970-197114May Day and Spring 1971 (Part 1)197115May Day 1971 (Part 2)197116Growing Police State1968-197117Cornell1968-197118Information on Ithaca1968-197119SDS Stuff, 1970-19711968-1971110-11SDS Housing Program, 1968-19691968-197121SDS: Student Power, ROTC, Open Admissions, Anti-Recruiting, Other Rallies, Internal Education, Etc., 1968-19691966-197122The Left, 1969-19701968-197023Washington, D.C., November 19691969-197024Syracuse, March 19, 1970197025New Haven, May 19701969-197026Movement, June-July 19701968-197027Ithaca, June-July 1970197028West Coast, August 1970197029Spring, 19721971-1972210Giap-Cabral; Ithaca United Action Against Imperialism; C-Town Riot(s); Winter-Spring, 197219671972211Giap-Cabral1972212Harrisburg1972213Fall, 19711970-1971214Cambodia, May Strike, 1970; Washington, May 8-9, 19701970215New American Movement1971216Miscellaneous1968-1971217Pamphlets: Liberation News Service; "What is Guerilla Theatre, Anyway?"; "The Destruction of Vancouver," Roy Stone; "Anarchist Peril," August Hamon; "No Treason: The Constitution of No Authority," Lysander Spooner; "Behind the Corporate Image ..."; "El Machete," Vol. 1, num 4; "Solidaire," no. 41965-1973218Publications: "Struggle!," vol. 1, no. 3; "How Many Vietnams..."; New Age"; "The Daily Californian," vol. 210, no. 30; "Augur," vol. II, no. 13; "The Rag," vol. 5, no. 19; "Berkeley Tribe," v. 5, no. 14, issue #941971-1972219Photographs1968-1970220Radical and SDS flyers1969-1972221TIOHERO/Glad Day Press: Publications Received197131TIOHERO/Glad Day Press: Publications Received1971-197232TIOHERO, Correspondence1970-197133TIOHERO, Miscellaneous197134TIOHERO1970-197135Volunteers1969-197036Various materials collected by Jean Dickson '72197237
Scope and Contents
Includes Glad Press items, material relating to Giap-Cabral Community and the Carpenter Hall takeover, Vietnam, Sharpeville Week, Apartheid in South Africa, America's War in Indo-China: The Last Phase by Committee of Concerned Asian Scholars at Cornell, Cornell, Corporations and War by the Ad hoc Committee to Combat Cornell Complicity, Cornell Aeronautical Lab, Ithaca Anti-War Coalition, Peoples Coalition for Peace and Justice, Ithaca United Action Against Imperialism, Peoples Anti-Fascist Resistance League, Southern African Liberation Committee. Poster for The Red Detachment of Women event sponsored by the New China Study Group, China Program Students. Daily Sun articles about Carpenter Hall takeover.Miscellaneous1968-197241Alternative Papers1970-197242Ithaca Labor Committee1968-197143Spring '72197244N.A.M. Spring 19721971-197245Davenport N.A.M. Conference1971-197246N.A.M.
